text:
8 Top Benefits of AI in Recruitment

Recruitment involves analyzing skills, experiences, verbal and non-verbal cues, personality traits, and matching candidates to job requirements. AI technologies including machine learning, automation, chatbots, and natural language processing are making it increasingly feasible.

How Does AI Work in Recruitment: AI assists rather than eliminates human recruiters. According to PwC research, 'by 2030, AI will have provided $15.7 trillion to the global economic growth.' AI automates mundane tasks, increasing efficiency.

AI capabilities include: Scanning CVs for specific skills using natural language processing; Understanding candidate body language and non-verbal gestures; Assessing personality traits against job requirements; Scheduling interviews through chatbots; Handling repetitive candidate questions; Keeping candidates engaged throughout recruitment.

Top 8 Benefits:
1. Faster Recruitment: AI automates repetitive tasks in minutes. Alorica's chatbot introduction saved '1200 man-hours during a period of about three months.'
2. Saves Hiring Costs: Reduced man-hours and optimized processes lower expenses. AI improves retention, reducing costly mis-hires that can cost '27 times the compensation of the mis-hired candidate.'
3. Quality Hiring: AI improves job descriptions, targets qualified candidates through programmatic advertising, and screens CVs.
4. No Overlooked Candidates: AI analyzes entire CVs to identify qualified candidates even without perfect keyword matching.
5. Reduces Human Biases: AI eliminates unconscious recruiter bias by identifying candidates based on skills and experience.
6. Better Candidate Experience: Research shows '58% of candidates turn down a company's offer because of their bad candidate experience.'
7. Better Employer Brand: According to LinkedIn, '80% of recruiters believe that employer branding has a crucial impact on the ability to hire the right candidates.'
8. Better Experience for Recruiters and Hiring Managers: AI handles job descriptions, sourcing, shortlisting, interview scheduling, and candidate engagement.
